level_signals: 6+ years of experience in consulting, strategy, business development, operations, technology, banking, analytics or related experience
JD internally refers to the role as 'Associate Manager level' and 'Sales Strategy Associate Manager'
Base pay range $125,800–$185,000 consistent with senior IC / manager level

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You have 6+ years of experience in consulting, strategy, business development, operations, technology, banking, analytics or related experience with a track record of leading initiatives and hitting goals
You have strong cross functional business experience, project management, and communication skills

You are comfortable working with large data sets + modeling in Excel / Google Sheets, advanced proficiency in SQL, and well-versed in data visualization tools (e.g. Sigma, Tableau) to help solve ambiguous problems
You are willing to roll up their sleeves and flex up or down as needed - "humble doer"
You have a Bachelor's degree or higher

You map complex operational workflows, diagnose data gaps, and architect AI-driven solutions end-to-end—from process discovery through prototype to production deployment. You build in AI tooling (e.g., Claude, ChatGPT, etc.) and ship real automation that eliminates manual work, closes visibility gaps, and accelerates decision-making across the business. You think in systems, not features. You treat every broken process as a build opportunity and every data gap as a use case waiting to be deployed. This is not an AI-curious role; this is an AI-native operating environment where you are expected to build, test, and iterate continuously.

scope.surface: Long-Tail SMB sales strategy and operations within Global Sales Vendor organization; focused on retaining and growing merchant partnerships at scale
scope.business_model: B2B marketplace; merchant (restaurant, grocer, convenience, retail) partnerships
primary_metrics_inferred: GTM efficiency across products and initiatives
core_responsibilities: Build and execute the Long-Tail Emerging Bets strategy for the Global Sales Vendor organization
Retain and grow Merchants at scale
Develop strategic vision for SMB Emerging Bets sales strategy aligned with DoorDash's business goals
Drive operational excellence in reporting cadences, pipeline tracking, and sales productivity
Partner with cross-functional teams to expand and elevate SMB offering
Guide top priorities for the US Marketplace and ensure GTM approach is effective and efficient
Plan for near and long-term with new products and new organizations

green_flags: Equity mentioned as part of compensation
Clear team and reporting structure described (reports to Manager of Revenue Strategy and Operations)
Expected fill date provided (7/4/26) suggesting active, real posting
Comprehensive benefits package including 16 weeks paid parental leave, 401(k) with match
red_flags: Location/work arrangement (remote, hybrid, onsite) is never stated in the JD body — significant ambiguity for candidates
JD title says 'Manager' but body repeatedly uses 'Associate Manager' — potential title/level inconsistency worth clarifying
AI-native requirement is unusually prescriptive and may be recently appended (tone shift mid-JD); scope of AI build expectation for a Sales S&O role is unclear

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from years requirement, comp range, and 'Associate Manager' language in body)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not directly stated in JD)
compensation.location_pay_differential (inferred from 'Base salary is localized according to an employee's work location')
ambiguities: Role title in posting is 'Manager, Sales Strategy & Operations' but the JD body repeatedly describes the role as 'Associate Manager level' and 'Sales Strategy Associate Manager' — the actual seniority level is ambiguous.
Work location/arrangement (remote, hybrid, onsite) is never specified in the JD body, creating significant ambiguity for applicants.

AI-native requirements paragraph reads as a distinctly different voice/style from rest of JD, suggesting it may have been appended; it is unclear whether building AI tooling is a primary or secondary responsibility.
